name: Strait of Hormuz
detail: Fully closed. Commercial traffic near zero. Heavily mined with active drone/missile threats. -15.0 mbpd disruption. @MarineTraffic AIS data on April 4 shows zero tanker transits through the strait.

name: Qatar LNG (Ras Laffan)
detail: Extensive damage from Mar 18-19 strikes. Force majeure declared. -2.0 mbpd equivalent disruption. No signs of repair activity per latest @QatarEnergy and @EnergyIntel updates.

name: Abqaiq (Saudi Arabia)
detail: Operating normally, but export constrained by Hormuz closure. East-West bypass active (+4.5 mbpd offset). Confirmed by @JavierBlas thread on April 3.

name: Kharg Island (Iran)
detail: Exporting estimated 1.2-1.4 mbpd via shadow fleet to China. Military targets struck but oil infrastructure currently spared. @OilSheppard reports continued limited shadow tanker activity.
update: Slight uptick in reported shadow fleet exports from prior 1.1-1.5 range.

name: Fujairah (UAE)
detail: Operations disrupted from mid-March drone strikes. Partial rerouting via Oman. @amaboraz notes insurance rates remain at war-risk levels with limited recovery.

name: Ras Tanura (Saudi Arabia)
detail: Refinery restarted but exports limited by Hormuz closure; rerouting to Yanbu. Latest satellite imagery analysis from @IntelSky confirms partial operations.

name: Assaluyeh / South Pars (Iran)
detail: Treatment plants struck mid-March; phases offline. @IranIntl_En reports no restart activity as of early April.

name: Bandar Abbas naval base
detail: Struck Mar 26 and Mar 28; IRGC Navy destroyed, pier damaged. @sentdefender posts on April 2 confirm no naval activity visible.

name: Kirkuk-Ceyhan Pipeline
detail: Flows at ~350 kbpd to Turkey's Ceyhan terminal under Iraq-KRG agreement. Could ramp to 450 kbpd. @AndrewMills1 confirms steady increases since Mar 17 restart.
update: Flows increased from initial ~250 kbpd to estimated 350 kbpd.

name: Bab el-Mandeb / Red Sea
detail: Houthi attacks intensified in solidarity with Iran. Most commercial shipping rerouting via Cape of Good Hope. @TheStudyofWar update on April 4 notes continued sporadic attacks.

name: Iranian Power Grid
detail: Isfahan, Bandar Abbas, Ahvaz power plants struck Mar 23. Estimated 35% of Iranian grid offline with partial restoration in some areas. Nationwide blackouts reported but improving slightly per @CriticalThreats.
update: Status revised from fully offline to degraded with some power restoration noted.

name: Fordow Enrichment Facility
detail: US strikes Mar 26 left facility ~60% offline. Deep underground facility partially survived but centrifuge operations severely disrupted. No new strikes reported by @BarakRavid on April 1.

name: Bandar Abbas Naval Base
detail: Cratered by US strikes Mar 26. Pier destroyed Mar 28 by B-2 strikes sinking 2 frigates. IRGC Navy effectively eliminated as fighting force. Confirmed by @Faytuks on April 3.

name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
detail: Not directly targeted in recent strikes. Continuing limited power generation despite national grid issues. Reported by @Reuters and @TheStudyofWar as of April 4.
update: Newly added facility impacted indirectly by the conflict.
